summary.intent: Complete QA for CHA-16 covering new crm.contacts.* RPC methods (list, getByExternal, getWithChannels) and post results to Linear.
summary.outcome: QA passed with health score 85→95. One medium auth coverage gap was found and fixed (commit 575adf1). Results posted to GitHub PR #13 instead of Linear due to missing auth token.

repo: Auth tests are organized per method group (cron, hooks, identity, senders) — new method groups need explicit read/write scope tests added to the auth test suite.
CRM RPC methods total 17 across crm.* namespace; all must be covered in READ_METHODS or equivalent auth registry.
NoopCrmService stubs exist alongside the CrmService trait — both need updating when adding new RPC methods.
Project uses nightly Rust toolchain (clippy and rustfmt run with nightly).

code.finding: QA plan file was modified as part of the session to record outcomes.

workflow: QA reports are written to .gstack/qa-reports/ within the workspace directory.
Linear auth token is not available in the shell environment — fallback is to post QA results as a GitHub PR comment.
Diff-aware QA scoping: only methods changed in the diff are tested, not the full RPC surface.

friction: Linear auth token not present in environment, blocking direct Linear comment posting — had to fall back to GitHub PR #13.
open_items: Linear QA comment was not posted; should be posted manually or the Linear token added to the environment for future sessions.
